title: Paramatan

{{Quote|The 'great oopik' of Paramatan is a modern day relic of its homeworld's by-gone age.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Paramatan''' was a primitive terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]] located in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim's]] [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]]. Its surface featured [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]] as well as plains and [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]]. The world's [[Species/Legends|fauna]] included [[Bird/Legends|birds]], [[Reptile/Legends|reptiles]], and [[great oopik]]s, a species having [[Reptavian/Legends|both]] avian and reptilian traits. The oopiks were a relic species of flightless creatures that made their home in the [[Paramatan fruit tree]]s of the continent of [[Grxy]]. Paramatan was discovered by [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] of the [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[House Elegin/Legends|House Elegin]] and eventually came to be used by the nobles of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region as a destination for [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] safaris.

==Description==
{{Quote|Few believed any had survived until, in the last decade, small clutches of oopiks have been discovered in remote forests on the southern continent of Grxy.|Holobrochure for the Tagge Holographic Museum of Chandrila|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
Paramatan was a terrestrial [[Planet/Legends|world]] with at least one [[Star/Legends|sun]]<ref name="Creatures" /> that was located in the [[Paramatan system/Legends|Paramatan system]] of the [[Senex sector/Legends|Senex sector]], a part of the [[Senex-Juvex/Legends|Senex-Juvex]] region in the [[Western Reaches/Legends|Western Reaches]] portion of the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. A [[Hyperspace route/Legends|hyperlane]] linked it to the [[Rulaar system/Legends|Rulaar system]].<ref name="TEA" />

Paramatan was a primitive world, with its surface being divided between deep [[Color/Legends|purple]] [[Ocean/Legends|oceans]] and continents featuring extensive plains and thick [[Forest/Legends|forests]] and [[Jungle/Legends|jungles]].<ref name="TEA" /> The forests of the southern continent [[Grxy]] were considered to be remote, and [[Paramatan fruit tree]]s grew there.<ref name="Creatures" />

Both [[Bird/Legends|avian]] and [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] [[Species/Legends|life forms]] were [[Homeworld/Legends|native]] to Paramatan, and the forests of Grxy were inhabited by small woodland creatures. Another example of the Grxy faunas was the [[great oopik]]. A flightless, [[Tree/Legends|arboreal]] creature utilizing ultrasound in [[Hunting/Legends|hunting]] its prey, the oopik had evolved from [[Reptavian/Legends|both]] birds and reptiles and were a relic species that represented an earlier age of its homeworld. Initially thought to have gone extinct more than a million [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[Imperial Era/Legends|reign]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the creatures were finally discovered on Grxy around the latter [[Time/Legends|time]] period.<ref name="Creatures" />

==History==
Paramatan was discovered by [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] affiliated with [[House Elegin/Legends|House Elegin]], an [[Noble/Legends|aristocratic]] [[Ancient Houses|Ancient House]] based on the nearby world of [[Caltinia]], as they were probing the periphery of the [[Thull's Shroud/Legends|Thull's Shroud]] [[Nebula/Legends|nebula]].<ref name="TEA" /> The planet was eventually mentioned in an entry of a [[Holo-brochure|holobrochure]] for the [[Tagge Holographic Museum]] of [[Chandrila/Legends|Chandrila]] dedicated to the great oopik.<ref name="Creatures" />

==Inhabitants==
Like the rest of the Senex-Juvex worlds, Paramatan was under the control of the region's aristocratic houses. It served as a hunting preserve used by [[Currency/Legends|affluent]] nobles, who went on safaris on the world and sometimes arranged for convenient accidents to happen there.<ref name="TEA" />

==Behind the scenes==
Paramatan was first mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', published by [[West End Games]] for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''. The section of the book mentioning the planet was written by [[Rick D. Stuart]] and illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the world in grid square L-17.<ref name="TEA" />
